purpose: Narrow static-gated review of SN-C3 physical scattering-matrix topology / phase-singularity observables. The review asks whether these sources touch the pre-spacetime static spine SG0-SG4, not whether they are merely interesting photonic scattering physics.

source_basis.title: Continuous Lines of Topological Singularities in Metasurface Scattering Matrices: From Nodal to Exceptional
source_basis.external_review_note: Source reports topological singularities/eigenvalue degeneracies in scattering matrices of open periodic photonic systems, protected by mirror symmetry, transforming from nodal to exceptional structures with loss, and appearing as vortex lines in cross-polarization scattering phase. It includes numerical and experimental metasurface demonstrations.

source_basis.title: Topological phase singularities in atomically thin high-refractive-index materials
source_basis.external_review_note: Source reports topological phase singularities in reflection of atomically thin high-refractive-index materials coupled to Fabry-Perot resonators; near reflection zeros, phase becomes singular with topological charge, and the effect is demonstrated for sensing/flat optics.

critical_review_prework.hard_bottleneck_attacked: whether physical scattering-matrix observables can honestly touch the pre-spacetime static residue spine, instead of winning by S-matrix/positive-geometry proximity or photonic topology language
thin_stick_risks: thin-stick risk: scattering-matrix eigenvalue topology treated as canonical readout
thin-stick risk: phase vortices treated as ordered residue signs
thin-stick risk: reflection zeros treated as Gr_+(0,n) terminal residues
thin-stick risk: metasurface or 2D-material experiments treated as substrate evidence
critical_review_prework.negative_progress_risk: Could become impressive photonics review with no actual SG0-SG4 contact.
critical_review_prework.why_not_convenient_drift: It directly tests whether SN-C3 survives the static-spine correction or should be demoted to negative-control comparator.

pg_gate_assessment.reason: Photonic/metasurface scattering systems provide physical wave carriers, but the carrier is not pre-spacetime geometry and is device/material dependent.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: carrier is ordinary electromagnetic response, not Formation Medium substrate

pg_gate_assessment.reason: Symmetry protection, scattering-matrix topology, reflection zeros, and topological charge impose constraints within the device/parameter space.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: constraints are photonic/material design constraints, not native positive-geometric admissibility

pg_gate_assessment.reason: The sources expose scattering response, phase topology, and wave interaction with open systems; they do not by themselves define relay/transport semantics.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: observable phase response replaces actual formation transport

pg_gate_assessment.reason: Topological singularity lines and phase singularities have identifiable structure in frequency/momentum/material parameter space, but this is not persistent support/channel identity in the Formation Medium sense.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: identity is parameter-tracked by the experimenter

pg_gate_assessment.reason: Scattering phase vortices and topological charge are physical observables, giving the strongest SN-C3 clue. However they are still optical/scattering observables, not canonical positive-geometric readout.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: external measurement phase is confused with intrinsic canonical form/readout

pg_gate_assessment.reason: Open systems and scattering matrices may compress bulk/material structure into boundary response, but the reviewed sources do not establish Formation-style non-faithful bulk/readout separation.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: readout remains a device response rather than non-faithful geometric projection

pg_gate_assessment.reason: Metasurface and atomically thin material platforms are experimentally accessible and have sensing/phase-engineering applications.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: harnessability becomes photonic device engineering, not Formation Medium access

pg_gate_assessment.reason: SN-C3 is useful as a negative control: if phase singularities cannot map to static residue gates, the source class remains photonic scattering topology only.
pg_gate_assessment.failure_mode: negative control is ignored because the sources sound geometrically suggestive

static_spine_gate_assessment.reason: Reflection zeros / scattering zeros are tempting analogues, but they are zeros of optical response functions or scattering operators, not terminal residue atoms with Omega=1.
static_spine_gate_assessment.required_before_upgrade: A precise map from response zero to terminal residue atom preserving canonical-form semantics, not merely zero amplitude or perfect absorption.

static_spine_gate_assessment.reason: The reviewed sources do not expose a static simplex residue tower with recursive facets Gr_+(1,n-1). They expose parameter-space topology of scattering/phase response.
static_spine_gate_assessment.required_before_upgrade: A source-backed or formal map from scattering observable strata to recursive Gr_+(1,n) boundary tower.

static_spine_gate_assessment.reason: Phase winding / topological charge has sign and loop-order flavor, but it is not ordered residue-route sign/coherence. Treating phase vortex charge as residue sign would be thin-stick leverage.
static_spine_gate_assessment.required_before_upgrade: An explicit comparison showing whether phase winding order implements, represents, or only mimics ordered residue signs.

static_spine_gate_assessment.reason: The sources do not identify local chart variables as products of Gr_+(1,2) interval blocks. Coupled-mode or scattering parameters are not automatically static interval factors.
static_spine_gate_assessment.required_before_upgrade: A finite model mapping scattering parameters or mode couplings to product static blocks with residue semantics.

static_spine_gate_assessment.reason: Changing frequency, momentum, loss, material, or device geometry is not mutation as transported static reflection. The reviewed sources do not show transport of static residue data through atlas transitions.
static_spine_gate_assessment.required_before_upgrade: A defined mutation-like transition preserving SG0-SG3 data, not merely tunable device parameters or exceptional/nodal transition.

red_team_conclusion.summary: SN-C3 has real physical observables and is useful for testing whether scattering-matrix topology can function as readout. But under SG0-SG4 it currently fails the core pre-spacetime static-spine gates. It should not be upgraded to Formation Medium-relevant beyond negative-control / weak observable-comparator status without a formal map from scattering observables to terminal atoms, static simplex recursion, ordered residue signs, product static blocks, and mutation transport.
red_team_conclusion.recommended_next: Compare SN-C3 against PRI-005 Ising machines on SG0-SG4: scattering observables may have stronger physical phase/topology readout, while Ising machines may have stronger combinatorial settling but also likely fail canonical residue semantics.

non_promotion_rule: This SN-C3 static-gated review is external-web-reviewed only. It does not ingest sources into the repo corpus, extract source claims, validate scattering-native physics, defeat Ising/high-resonance comparison, select a substrate, prove a settling law, or establish engineering readiness.
